mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with decreases in FEDFUNDS over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points within a single month signals weakening aggregate demand and triggers downward earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in one month over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and industrial production indicate a coordinated contraction in both consumer demand and manufacturing output, amplifying recession risk and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month and INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of months where both RSAFS and INDPRO decline month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a rising federal funds rate increases real borrowing costs, compresses equity valuations, and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of high-inflation months where FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding corporate output and revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with monthly increases in INDPRO over a 2-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1500 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and labor, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1500 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: A decrease in payroll employment combined with a rising unemployment rate confirms labor market deterioration, reducing household income expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases month-over-month and UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS decreases and UNRATE increases simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth reflects strengthening aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not positively correlated with quarter-over-quarter increases in GDPC1 over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).
